channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ADC Sets Dates for Ward, Local Government, State Congresses and April 14 National Convention
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) don announce say dem go hold their National Convention on April 14, 2026, as part of effort to restructure the party leadership nationwide. According to the offic...

item.title: Public to vote on artwork created from timber of felled Sycamore Gap tree
item.description: The National Trust is inviting the public to help decide which artist will create an artwork from the wood of the Sycamore Gap tree, which was illegally felled in September 2023. The iconic tree, loca...

item.title: Three Iranian Women’s Football Players Return Home After Humanitarian Visa Decision
item.description: Three members of Iran’s women’s football team, who were previously granted humanitarian visas to stay in Australia, have decided to return to Iran. This decision follows their original choice to remai...

item.title: Kenya keeps fuel prices steady despite Middle East tensions
item.description: Kenyans can breathe a sigh of relief as fuel prices in the country remain unchanged for the coming month. The Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (EPRA) announced that the retail prices of petro...

item.title: Families and Community Mourn Seven People Killed in Lake Tanganyika Boat Accident
item.description: On Friday morning, a tragic boat accident occurred on Lake Tanganyika near Kalalangabo village, Kigoma Region, leaving seven people dead, including four dedicated health workers from Kigoma District C...

item.title: Judith Thompson revisits themes of ageing and family in Queen Maeve at Tarragon Theatre
item.description: Judith Thompson returns to the Tarragon Theatre with her latest play, Queen Maeve, marking a homecoming for the celebrated Canadian playwright. The play centres on Mrs. Nurmi, an elderly woman residin...

item.title: NSTF Brilliants Programme celebrates top matric maths and science performers to boost South Africa's STEM future
item.description: South Africa grapples with serious challenges in education, particularly in mathematics and physical science, as shown by declining scores in international studies like TIMSS 2023, where the country r...

item.title: Former actor John Alford dies in prison two months into sentence for sexual assaults on teenage girls
item.description: John Alford, the 54-year-old actor known for his roles in the BBC children's series Grange Hill and the ITV drama London's Burning in the 1990s, has died in prison. His real name was John Shannon. He ...

item.title: Madhya Pradesh HC to Hear Objections on ASI Report Regarding Bhojshala-Kamal Maula Mosque
item.description: The Madhya Pradesh High Court, Indore bench, is scheduled to hear on Monday the objections and suggestions related to the Archaeological Survey of India's (ASI) report on the disputed Bhojshala-Kamal ...

item.title: Canada wins first wheelchair curling Paralympic gold since 2014
item.description: Canada's wheelchair curling team claimed gold at the Milano-Cortina 2026 Paralympic Games after defeating China 4-3 in a tense final. This victory marks Canada's first gold medal in the event since 20...

item.title: Estimating Pi Using Buffon's Needle Probability Experiment
item.description: March 14, known as Pi Day, celebrates the mathematical constant pi, which is the ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter and appears in various scientific and artistic contexts. While humans...

item.title: Raphael Tuju Alleges Police Eviction from Karen Property and Child Injury
item.description: Former Cabinet Secretary Raphael Tuju claims that police officers stormed his property in Karen at around 3am, forcing him and his family out in a dramatic raid. He says more than 50 officers arrived ...

item.title: Jason Dy Performs as Elton John on Your Face Sounds Familiar Week 12
item.description: In the latest episode of the popular ABS-CBN singing impersonation show 'Your Face Sounds Familiar,' Jason Dy took on the iconic persona of Elton John during Week 12 of the competition. Known for his ...

item.title: Several Iranian women’s soccer players in Australia choose to return home
item.description: The number of Iranian women’s soccer players remaining in Australia is rapidly declining as three more players who had initially sought asylum have now decided to return to Iran. Originally, seven mem...

item.title: Two People Die Following Shooting in Winnipeg’s North End
item.description: Two individuals, a man in his 30s and a teenage boy, were killed in a shooting in Winnipeg’s North End earlier this week. The incident occurred around 5:30 p.m. on Thursday in the 300 block of Boyd Av...

item.title: Durban implements water rationing and stabilisation measures after Nagle Dam valve failure
item.description: The eThekwini Municipality, in partnership with uMngeni-uThukela Water, has introduced a water rationing and stabilisation plan to protect Durban's water supply following a failure of the control valv...

item.title: Presco Plc to invest $200 million to expand oil palm plantations in Abia State
item.description: Presco Plc, wey be one of the major agro-industrial oil palm companies for West Africa, don announce say dem go invest $200 million for Abia State oil palm sector. The Chairperson, Olakanmi Sarumi, ta...

item.title: Democrats Consider Military Veterans for 2028 Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: In the wake of escalating U.S. military operations against Iran under President Donald Trump, Democrats are debating whether their 2028 presidential nominee should be a military veteran. The Politico ...

item.title: Trump urges allies to reopen Strait of Hormuz amid rising Middle East tensions; UN calls for ceasefire in Lebanon
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East have escalated as US President Donald Trump called for allied nations to deploy warships to the Strait of Hormuz following US strikes on Iran’s Kharg Island, a key oil expo...

item.title: Ollur Constituency Likely to See Tight Contest in Upcoming Kerala Assembly Elections
item.description: Ollur in Thrissur district is considered a bellwether constituency in Kerala politics, having consistently elected candidates from the front that ultimately formed the state government since 1982. The...
